features.title: Read MCP Registry and local catalog metadata
features.summary: Normalizes remotes, packages, headers, registryType and transport without requiring users to choose a server type.
features.nextStep: Keep registry parsing covered with held-out registry fixtures.

features.title: Downloaded package metadata corpus for popular MCP servers
features.summary: Uses npm pack and pip download metadata in sandbox to produce stable golden scenarios without executing foreign server code.
features.nextStep: Refresh corpus versions before release trains; do not ship downloaded tarballs/wheels.

features.title: Model initialize/tools-list evidence as classifier input
features.summary: Auto mode records tool names, annotations, resources/prompts and transport capability hints as evidence for runtime classification.
features.nextStep: Extend lab with live subprocess sandbox traces when a safe OS sandbox is present.

features.title: Infer runtimeType automatically
features.summary: Classifies stateless, stateful, external, interactive, side-effecting, legacy and unknown surfaces from metadata plus probe evidence.
features.nextStep: Reject policy relaxations unless a golden fixture demonstrates the evidence.

features.title: Infer stateClass automatically
features.summary: Separates stateless, session-stateful, project-stateful, credential-stateful, remote-session-stateful, host-stateful and unknown-conservative.
features.nextStep: Add more credential-scoped SaaS fixtures as registry coverage grows.

features.title: Infer effectClass automatically
features.summary: Separates read-only, external-read, ephemeral-state, project-mutating, external-mutating, host-mutating, process-exec and unknown.
features.nextStep: Treat untrusted tool annotations as hints only; keep conservative fallbacks.

features.title: Plan safe concurrency policy from inferred classes
features.summary: Maps state/effect evidence into multi-reader, single-session, single-writer, isolated-per-project or plan-only behavior.
features.nextStep: Keep maxWorkers=1 for single-writer/project-isolated policies.

features.title: Auto-install only approved/trusted candidates
features.summary: Approved/trusted servers can be installed automatically; review/unknown/blocked remain plan-only unless policy explicitly allows them.
features.nextStep: Wire optional signatures/SBOM checks when registry metadata exposes them consistently.

features.title: Random unknown servers stay plan-only
features.summary: Unknown random registry or command candidates are discovered and classified conservatively but not silently executed.
features.nextStep: Add an explicit local sandbox executor before any unknown-code dry run is allowed.

features.title: Probe after approved install
features.summary: When enabled, auto mode refreshes live tool evidence after adding a server so scheduling is based on observed surfaces.
features.nextStep: Persist probe failure reasons in the dashboard.

features.title: Human-readable lab evidence report
features.summary: mcpace advanced dev lab defaults to report and shows server -> evidence -> inferred classes -> policy.
features.nextStep: Render the same evidence matrix in the dashboard.

features.title: No sandbox package/download artifacts in release bundle
features.summary: Release includes fixtures and reports, not npm tarballs, PyPI wheels, caches, mirrors or sandbox-specific URLs.
features.nextStep: Keep package downloads outside the repository tree.

features.title: Wide npm/PyPI metadata sweep for popular and random MCP servers
features.summary: Records 37 npm packages and 4 PyPI distributions as metadata-only evidence buckets, including browser, SaaS, database, documentation, cluster and wallet classes.
features.nextStep: Refresh the sweep before releases and keep downloaded packages outside the repository.

features.title: Use multiple metadata layers before choosing runtime type
features.summary: Each lab scenario records the metadata layers used: registry server.json, package registry metadata, package artifact manifests, README/keyword scans, safe tools/list probes and explicit trust boundaries.
features.nextStep: Persist the same evidence ledger from live mcpace advanced server auto runs into the dashboard.

features.title: Separate local browser sessions, remote browser sessions and browser data lookup
features.summary: Classifies Playwright/Puppeteer/Chrome/DevTools/local browser bridges as host-stateful, Browserbase/BrowserStack-style providers as remote-session-stateful, and caniuse/docs as external-read.
features.nextStep: Add live sandbox startup traces when an OS-level sandbox is available.

features.title: Split external read APIs from SaaS/admin mutating APIs
features.summary: Docs/search/maps/caniuse/context lookup use budgeted multi-reader, while Notion/Sentry/Slack/GitHub/Kubernetes/Heroku/Azure/Wallet APIs remain credential-scoped single-writer.
features.nextStep: Add registry-provided tool annotations as hints only when the server is trusted.

features.title: Separate file/project databases from network database connections
features.summary: SQLite/file DB remains project-scoped; Postgres/Redis/Supabase-style connections become credential/database scoped single-writer.
features.nextStep: Expose database connection conflict domains in mcpace advanced server instances output.

features.title: Handle project-local read-only analysis tools
features.summary: Tools like ESLint are project-scoped because config/files matter, but their default effect class is read-only rather than project-mutating.
features.nextStep: Add language-server/indexer fixtures when package metadata is available.

features.title: Held-out random browser package negative set
features.summary: Random browser packages from npm search are metadata-classified but plan-only, proving auto mode detects type without silently executing unknown code.
features.nextStep: Rotate held-out random samples periodically.

features.title: Random 500 npm MCP metadata sweep
features.summary: Records a deterministic held-out sweep of 500 npm MCP candidates. The sweep separates cases that can be classified from search/package metadata from cases that must remain unknown-conservative until a safe initialize/tools-list probe is available.
features.nextStep: Use safe MCP initialize/tools-list probes to reduce the unknown-conservative bucket instead of guessing from package names.
